title: The History of Port Arthur
australian_relation: Exact ABC public media items naming WA and TAS haunted historic sites.
coordinate_evidence_note: ABC text names `Port Arthur` in a ghost/supernatural context; gazetteer result state-checked as Port Arthur Historic Site, Port Arthur, Tasmania, 7182, Australia.
evidence_summary: The History of Port Arthur It's all gaol, ghosts and gothic allure as we delve into the dark history of our most infamous penal colony, Port Arthur. Operating between 1830-1877, it had the reputation for being the destination for the hardest of convicts. Port Arthur historian Andrew Smith joins Trevor for a unique insight into Tasmania's top tourist attraction. In 1872 English novelist Anthony Trollope claimed that no man desired to see the "strange ruins" of Tasmania's Port Arthur. Was he wrong?
